channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Ethics investigation of state Senate president prompts conflict-of-interest rules scrutiny
item.description: Attorney and former State DFL House Majority Leader Ryan Winkler joined MPR News host Cathy Wurzer on Morning Edition on Thursday to talk about conflicts of interest at the Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Hope Florida Foundation meeting disrupted by slurs, pornographic images amid scrutiny
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) – Pornographic images and racial slurs abruptly ended the virtual Hope Florida Foundation meeting Thursday morning. Board members then reconvened later in the afternoon for an hour and 30 minutes.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Letitia James launches high-dollar fundraiser after alleged mortgage fraud case
item.description: New York Attorney General Letitia James launches an ActBlue fundraiser seeking donations up to $18K-per-person in wake of alleged mortgage fraud.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nearly 90% of Consumer Financial Protection Bureau cut as Trump's government downsizing continues
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is drastically shrinking the workforce and mission of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, eviscerating an agency created after the Great Recession with the goal of protecting Americans from fraud, abuse and deceptive practices.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Draft budget plan proposes deep cuts across federal health programs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A draft budget proposal circulating among federal officials would dramatically deepen cuts at the nation's top health agency, eliminating some public health programs entirely and serving as a roadmap for more mass firings. The document suggests a cut in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Pentagon spokesman tied to online DEI purge was asked to resign, official says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former Pentagon spokesman John Ullyot was asked to resign this week, a senior defense official told The Associated Press on Thursday, in the latest shakeup for the Defense Department following firings and other changes under President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump admin imposes sanctions against Bank of Yemen for supporting Houthis
item.description: The Trump administration imposed sanctions against the International Bank of Yemen and several of its leaders for allegedly aiding Houthis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Politics Friday: Digging into Minnesota‘s political history
item.description: Friday at noon, MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst and a team of MPR News reporters and producers share a bit Minnesota political history with some modern twists.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Letitia James reaps what she sows after leveling 'laughable' case at Trump: expert
item.description: A federal housing agency sent a criminal referral to the Department of Justice Monday arguing New York Attorney General Letitia James appeared to have falsified mortgage records.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Afghanistan Medal of Honor recipient reenlists in Marine Corps reserves after 15 years
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Dakota Meyer, a Marine who was awarded the Medal of Honor for heroism in the Afghanistan War but later became a sharp critic of the Biden administration over its chaotic withdrawal from that conflict, has reenlisted in the military and will serve in the Marine Reserves.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Appeals court denies DOJ bid to block return of Kilmar Abrego Garcia from El Salvador prison
item.description: A federal court rejected a DOJ bid to block the return of Maryland illegal immigrant Kilmar Abrego Garcia from El Salvador, warning the Trump administration must not override due process or rule of law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court to hear oral arguments in birthright citizenship case
item.description: The Supreme Court is set to hear arguments in May over President Donald Trump’s executive order aimed at ending birthright citizenship for children of noncitizens.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court to hear rare May arguments in birthright citizenship battle
item.description: The Supreme Court scheduled a rare May oral argument on the Trump administration’s emergency request to narrow a series of nationwide blocks on President Trump’s executive order that would restrict birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: At contentious Wisconsin elections meeting, Democratic chair threatens to oust Republican official
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin Elections Commission discussion of uncounted ballots in Madison devolved into a shouting match Thursday, with the Democratic chair threatening to kick a Republican commissioner out of the meeting.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Barack Obama, Bernie Sanders, Ben Carson among most liked politicians, surveys show
item.description: In one YouGov survey on Democratic figures, 58 percent of respondents said they felt “very favorable” or “somewhat favorable” toward Obama.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Republican uses George Washington, American Revolution to reject notion Ukraine should surrender to Russia
item.description: Rep. Don Bacon pointed to the American Revolution to push back against the idea that Ukraine should surrender to Russia. Bacon has advocated for the U.S. and Europe to "arm Ukraine to the max.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FLASHBACK: Liberals are fine with Harvard getting a tax break but were against it for a Christian college
item.description: The Trump administration is urging the IRS to revoke Harvard’s tax-exempt status over alleged campus antisemitism, and they could have grounds under this Reagan-era SCOTUS ruling.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SCOOP: Pence urges Republicans to hold the line on tax hikes for the rich as Trump weighs options
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence told House Republicans to "stand firm" and make the "Trump-Pence" tax cuts permanent as the White House floats a millionaire tax hike.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump policies top the talking points at Minnesota lawmaker town halls
item.description: Lawmakers from both parties fielded questions and comments from constituents in person. President Donald Trump’s policies on immigration, health care and budget cuts generated the most heat.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, Dan Kraker, and Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Burchett signals support for sending US criminals to El Salvador
item.description: Rep. Tim Burchett (R-Tenn.) on Wednesday defended President Donald Trump’s suggestion that U.S. citizens convicted of violent crimes be sent to Salvadoran prisons.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans consider increasing taxes on the rich in break from party orthodoxy
item.description: Republicans in Congress are considering increasing taxes on the rich as a part of President Trump’s “big, beautiful bill” of ambitious legislative priorities, a striking development that breaks with decades of party orthodoxy and is spurring alarm bells from traditional conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Trump says trade deals could be done within a month
item.description: After saying earlier in the day that he was “in no rush” to finish trade deals, President Donald Trump said he thought he could wrap up tariff talks “over the next three or four weeks.”.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former DeSantis spokesperson accused of exposing himself at mall
item.description: A New College of Florida official and former spokesperson for Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis was arrested Tuesday and charged with exposure of sexual organs.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: Trump joins tariff talks with Japan as US seeks deals amid trade wars
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday inserted himself directly into trade talks with Japanese officials, a sign of the high stakes for the United States after its tariffs rattled the economy and caused the administration to assure the public that it would quickly reach deals.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, MARI YAMAGUCHI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge finds cause to hold Trump administration in criminal contempt for violating deportation order
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday said he has found probable cause to hold the Trump administration in criminal contempt of court and warned he could seek officials' prosecution for violating his orders last month to turn around planes carrying deportees to an El Salvador prison.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What happens next after judge warns of possible contempt prosecution over deportation flights order
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge's ruling that the Trump administration appears to have willfully violated his order to turn around planes of migrants headed for El Salvador increases the prospect of officials being held in criminal contempt of court and potentially facing possible prosecution. U.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, MARK SHERMAN and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Protest of Lombardo's 'freeloaders' comment planned Saturday in downtown Las Vegas
item.description: A rally planned Saturday afternoon in downtown Las Vegas is stepping up the pushback against Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo's remarks that freeloaders are behind opposition to policy changes.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Retail sales jump as people rush to buy cars ahead of tariffs
item.description: The key driver of the economy saw the biggest increase in over two years in March, as car buyers tried to get ahead of President Donald Trump's tariffs on imported autos and auto parts.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh, MPR News

item.title: Biden alleges Trump has 'taken a hatchet' to Social Security in his first post-presidency speech
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Former President Joe Biden warned on Tuesday that President Donald Trump has “taken a hatchet” to Social Security, weighing in on a critical issue for millions of Americans as the 82-year-old Democrat briefly returned to the national stage.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signs executive action to lower drug prices
item.description: The action is intended to build upon the existing program for Medicare drug price negotiations, which was created by the Inflation Reduction Act that passed during the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Lupkin, MPR News

item.title: Biden says Trump administration has done 'breathtaking' damage to Social Security
item.description: Former President Joe Biden warned a Chicago audience assembled Tuesday at the national conference of Advocates, Counselors and Representatives for the Disabled that the Trump administration has "taken a hatchet" to Social Security.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: Illinois Democrats mull next moves after strong showing in April elections
item.description: Gov. JB Pritzker and top state Democrats gathered Monday at a union hall in DuPage County to celebrate the party's near clean sweep of Illinois municipal elections two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Former President Joe Biden to speak in Chicago on Tuesday in effort to rally support for Social Security
item.description: Former President Joe Biden is scheduled to speak at the national conference of Advocates, Counselors and Representatives for the Disabled.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: Trump urges Congress to make daylight saving time permanent
item.description: President Donald Trump said Congress should “push hard for more Daylight at the end of a day” in a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Florida education bill sparks debate over funds for advanced placement programs
item.description: Florida lawmakers battled it out on the House floor this week over a bill that could lead to significant funding cuts to education.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Deadline arrives as Nevada lawmakers work furiously to pass bills out of committee
item.description: A deadline today will be the death knell for some of the nearly 1,100 bills in the Nevada Legislature. Some of the bills never went to a vote. Others were dead on arrival, identified by Gov. Joe Lombardo as bills he would veto.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump eyes major cuts to NOAA research
item.description: The Trump administration is eyeing cuts to climate, weather and ocean research in a draft budget blueprint for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5
